message:
test(cli_search_semantic_flags): upgrade 3 .is_some() asserts to pin exact SearchMode

Per the testing-hardening sweep, three tests in
tests/cli_search_semantic_flags.rs were checking that CLI-parsed
search modes were merely `.is_some()` without verifying the parsed
value matched what the `--mode <X>` flag requested. A regression
where `--mode semantic` silently parsed to `Some(SearchMode::Lexical)`
(or vice versa) would have slipped past all three assertions while
still passing clap's basic `Option<T>` population.

Upgraded assertions:

1. search_combines_mode_and_model_flags (was line 162)
   `--mode semantic --model minilm`
   old: assert!(mode.is_some())
   new: assert_eq!(mode, Some(SearchMode::Semantic), ...)

2. search_combines_mode_semantic_and_approximate (was line 238)
   `--mode semantic --approximate`
   old: assert!(mode.is_some())
   new: assert_eq!(mode, Some(SearchMode::Semantic), ...)

3. search_combines_mode_hybrid_and_approximate (was line 260)
   `--mode hybrid --approximate`
   old: assert!(mode.is_some())
   new: assert_eq!(mode, Some(SearchMode::Hybrid), ...)

Each assertion now includes a diagnostic message naming the
expected variant so a regression's failure output points straight
at the flag-vs-variant mismatch. SearchMode was already imported
at the top of the file so no new dependency is introduced.

Validation: all 3 upgraded tests pass locally via
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane3 cargo test --test
cli_search_semantic_flags`. No production code changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
